meta.title: Poly Robot Machine Mode Contract
meta.description: Decision brief for making Robot Mode a shared Poly/SDK operation envelope across CLI, MCP, HTTP, SDK, and generated clients.

props.title: Poly Robot Machine Mode Contract
props.subtitle: Robot Mode should be a shared operation envelope, not a CLI formatting flag.

props.content: The selected direction is to define a versioned PolyOperationEnvelope in the shared domain layer, then project it through CLI, MCP, HTTP, SDK, generated clients, FlowMind, and replay. Provider auth stays adapter-owned. Evidence stays owner-local with a shared reference index.

props.label: ROOT DECISION
props.title: Take the Contract, Not Another Surface
Selected
props.content: Introduce PolyOperationEnvelope in @lev-os/domain. Poly owns builders, validators, renderers, and projection metadata. CLI, MCP, HTTP, SDK, generated clients, FlowMind, and replay all project the same semantics.

props.title: Rejected
props.content: Do not make Robot Mode CLI-first. Do not promote ExecutionRunResponse as the universal root. Do not let CommandRunResult become the machine contract. Those options optimize for local convenience and lose long-term parity.

props.label: CONTRACT
props.title: V1 Envelope Shape
props.subtitle: Keep the runtime path typed until a protocol, receipt, replay, or CLI boundary needs JSON.

props.content:
poly_operation_envelope:
  version: 1
  operation:
    id: string
    service: string
    method: string
    surface: cli | sdk | mcp | http | grpc | flowmind | replay
    request_id: string
    idempotency_key: string?
    caller: string?
  side_effect:
    class: read | write | execute | auth | bootstrap | diagnostics
    dry_run: boolean
    preview: object?
    confirmation: object?
  policy:
    decision: allowed | denied | needs_human
    redaction_profile: string?
    approval_ref: string?
    reason: string?
  result:
    status: ok | failed | timeout | cancelled | partial | not_implemented
    data: object?
    error:
      code: string
      message: string
      remediation: string?
    exit_code: number?
  evidence:
    run_id: string?
    correlation_id: string?
    receipt_refs: string[]
    event_refs: string[]
    artifact_refs: string[]
    proof_refs: string[]

props.label: BOUNDARIES
props.title: Auth, Evidence, and Policy Stay Explicit
Provider Auth
props.content: Provider auth is a platform/plugin adapter capability family: provider.auth.status, provider.auth.bootstrap, provider.auth.refresh, and provider.accounts.select. Poly core never owns credentials, cookies, browser profiles, or account secrets.

props.title: Evidence Graph
props.content: Evidence stays owner-local and immutable. The shared layer owns a small append-only index with refs, hashes, owners, redaction profile, and created time. Envelopes carry refs, not bulky proof payloads.

props.title: Policy State
props.content: Human fallback is a typed policy state. needs_human is not an exception, process failure, or stderr string. Redaction applies before content-bearing projections.

props.label: PROOF
props.title: Fail-Closed Conformance Gates
props.subtitle: Promotion is blocked unless semantic parity holds across every tested binder.

props.title: Envelope schema
props.content: Owner: core/domain. Blocks promotion if identity, policy, side-effect declaration, typed result/error, or evidence refs are missing.

props.title: Projection builders
props.content: Owner: core/poly. Blocks promotion on surface drift, generic wrappers, unstable exit mapping, or inconsistent renderer behavior.

props.title: First plugin proofs
props.content: Owners: plugins/dna and plugins/deploy. Proves dna validate/compile plus deploy static --dry-run without network or live writes.

props.title: Pentagon parity
props.content: Owner: core/testing. Blocks promotion when CLI, MCP, HTTP, SDK, or generated clients diverge semantically.

props.title: UltraQA attacks
props.content: Owner: UltraQA suite. Blocks redaction leaks, hostile argument bypasses, dry-run truth failures, and large-output envelope copies.

props.title: Operation Envelope Projection Path
props.content:
flowchart LR
  Caller[Caller or agent] --> Envelope[PolyOperationEnvelope]
  Envelope --> CLI[CLI projection]
  Envelope --> MCP[MCP tool]
  Envelope --> HTTP[HTTP/OpenAPI]
  Envelope --> SDK[SDK and generated client]
  Envelope --> Policy[Policy and redaction]
  Policy --> Adapter[Execution or adapter capability]
  Adapter --> Evidence[Owner-local evidence refs]
  Evidence --> Proof[Pentagon and UltraQA gates]

props.title: 1. Domain
props.content: Add PolyOperationEnvelope and supporting enums/refs in @lev-os/domain.

props.title: 2. Poly
props.content: Add builders, validators, renderers, and projection metadata around existing handler results.

props.title: 3. Root CLI
props.content: Build the envelope internally, then render human text, JSON, or plain output at the final boundary.

props.title: 4. Plugin Proofs
props.content: Adapt dna validate/compile and deploy static --dry-run as the first semantic proof slices.

props.title: 5. Binders
props.content: Project the same operation metadata through MCP, HTTP/OpenAPI, SDK helpers, and generated clients.

props.title: 6. Gates
props.content: Run Pentagon and UltraQA after real success/failure fixtures exist.

props.title: CLI Overfit
props.content: If --json becomes the architecture root, MCP/API/SDK parity will be bolted on later and tested weakly.

props.title: Credential Ownership
props.content: Provider auth must expose opaque refs and needs_human state. Poly core must not become a browser profile or credential store.

props.title: Log Index Trap
props.content: The evidence layer is a proof index, not a transcript dump. Large content stays behind refs and redaction gates.
